steps.title: Identify the clause structure
steps.content: After the introductory infinitive phrase, the sentence contains a subject noun phrase (embryos inside African turquoise killifish eggs) followed by the blank, where the main verb belongs.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content: The main verb of an independent clause must be a finite verb—a conjugated form matching the subject. enter is a finite verb that agrees with embryos. The other options are non-finite forms: to enter (infinitive), having entered (perfect participle), and entering (present participle)—none work as main verbs. That matches choice A — A is correct.
